quotes: Write loyal cantons of contemned love.

records.ety: [F. canton, augm. of OF. cant edge, corner. See 1st Cant.]

defs: A small portion; a division; a compartment.
quotes: That little canton of land called the “English pale”
There is another piece of Holbein's, . . . in which, in six several cantons, the several parts of our Savior's passion are represented.

defs: A small territorial district; esp. one of the twenty-two independent states which form the Swiss federal republic; in France, a subdivision of an arrondissement. See Arrondissement.
A division of a shield occupying one third part of the chief, usually on the dexter side, formed by a perpendicular line from the top of the shield, meeting a horizontal line from the side.
quotes: The king gave us the arms of England to be borne in a canton in our arms.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cantoned ; p. pr. & vb. n. Cantoning.]

defs: To divide into small parts or districts; to mark off or separate, as a distinct portion or division.
quotes: They canton out themselves a little Goshen in the intellectual world.

defs: To allot separate quarters to, as to different parts or divisions of an army or body of troops.
